question.text: What does Clause 8.4 of the IFSCA (AML, CFT and KYC) Guidelines mandate regarding certification?

answer.text: Officers and employees of Regulated Entities dealing with AML/CFT matters must obtain relevant NISM-IFSCA certification
Only the Principal Officer needs to be certified by NISM
Certification is optional and recommended but not mandatory
Only the Governing Body members need AML/CFT certification

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Clause 8.4 of the IFSCA (AML, CFT and KYC) Guidelines mandates that officers and employees of Regulated Entities who deal with AML/CFT matters must obtain the relevant NISM-IFSCA certification. This is the very certification (NISM-IFSCA-01) that this exam is for. Note: This requirement comes from the IFSCA Guidelines source document; the workbook references this mandate indirectly through its discussion of training and certification obligations.
**The Trap:** The certification is MANDATORY, not optional. It applies to all relevant officers and employees dealing with AML/CFT, not just the Principal Officer.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This is unique to IFSCA-01 — the certification requirement is self-referential. Series XXIV has its own certification mandate under SEBI regulations. Understanding WHY you are taking this exam is itself an exam-worthy topic.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** You are taking the NISM-IFSCA-01 exam precisely because of Clause 8.4. This certification is a regulatory requirement for anyone dealing with AML/CFT matters in IFSC-regulated entities.
